purpose: Author the 8 required e2e scenarios (AC-SCEN-1..8) plus AC-REMOVE-4/AC-PURGE-5/AC-UNIV-1 that exercise the already-implemented S1-S10 pending-direct-invite-marker and universal-removal machinery end-to-end through the REAL app UI, relay, and MLS protocol. This story introduces zero new production logic and zero mocks — it is a pure consumer of every module S1-S10 built, verifying the whole-flow guarantees architecture.md's 'Order-Sensitive Composition' section lists (marker-implies-pending-or-gone, purge-on-removal is commit-independent, no stuck-unremovable state, orphan-marker safety) at the black-box/UI level rather than in unit tests.
dependencies_forbidden: no edits to any S1-S10-owned production file
no page.route() interception or fixture-injected fake relay responses
no raw WebSocket publish except the one documented AC-SCEN-2 exception (helpers/namelessJoinRequestFixture.ts), mirroring the sole prior precedent (helpers/forgedPairingAck.ts)
external_ports: strfry relay (ws://localhost:7777, Docker, real MLS/NIP-59 traffic through the app's own publish paths)
events_consumed: PendingDirectInviteMarkerSet (consumed via getPendingDirectInvites, observed as Cancel Invite / Remove Member row affordance)
CancelPendingInvitationResult (consumed via the shared performGroupMemberRemoval removal path, observed as row disappearance)
DeleteMemberProfileFn (consumed indirectly — observed via the re-invite-after-removal label transition, the only externally observable proxy for the purge)
